title: Contrastive Vision-Language Alignment Makes Efficient Instruction Learner
key_findings: Using only 10% instruction tuning data, reaches 95% performance of state-of-the-art method LLaVA on zero-shot ScienceQA-Image benchmark
Proposes a contrastive vision-language alignment approach that is more efficient than generative alignment methods
Addresses weak alignment between vision and language modalities in existing methods

problem.description: Existing vision-language instruction following models require vision-language alignment mechanisms, but current generative alignment methods show weak alignment between modalities, making models data-hungry
challenges: High-quality instruction data is hard to collect due to time-consuming process
Existing methods (LLaVA, LLaMA-Adapter) use generative alignment with image captioning loss
